channel.title: Frontion News Headlines
channel.description: Geopolitical news headlines from Tier 1 sources

item.title: 🇺🇸🇮🇷 Trump Rejects Iran Ceasefire Response as "Totally Unacceptable"
item.description: US President dismissed Iran's response to a US ceasefire proposal designed to end the war. The Strait of Hormuz remains effectively shut, severely disrupting global energy shipments as oil prices jumped on the news.

item.title: 🇺🇦🇷🇺 Russia Breaches Three-Day Ukraine Ceasefire, Zelenskyy Says
item.description: Ukrainian President Zelenskyy accused Russia of violating a three-day ceasefire agreement, as Putin publicly suggested the conflict may be "coming to an end" while condemning Western support for Ukraine.

item.title: 🇬🇧🏥 British Paratroopers Land on Remote Island for Hantavirus Case
item.description: A specialist British military team parachuted onto Tristan da Cunha, one of the world's most remote inhabited islands, to treat a suspected hantavirus case. The operation highlights global health concerns as a cruise ship outbreak continues.

item.title: 🇱🇧⚔️ Medics Among 51 Killed in Israeli Attacks on Lebanon
item.description: Israeli attacks intensify in southern Lebanon, with 552 killed since the ceasefire started on April 16. Medical personnel are among the casualties as the conflict continues to escalate.

item.title: 🇵🇱🇭🇺 Polish Ex-Minister Flees Hungary to US After PM Warning
item.description: Zbigniew Ziobro, former Polish justice minister accused of leading an organized criminal enterprise, fled to the US after Hungary's new PM said the country would not protect people wanted elsewhere.

item.title: 🇮🇱🇮🇶 Israel Operated Secret Military Base in Iraq During Iran War
item.description: Israel established a clandestine outpost in the Iraqi desert to support its air campaign against Iran, housing special forces and serving as a logistics hub. Israel launched airstrikes against Iraqi troops who nearly discovered it early in the war.

item.title: 🇮🇷 Iran's Supreme Leader Missing and Silent as War Negotiations Continue
item.description: Mojtaba Khamenei has not appeared publicly since being severely injured in a February airstrike that killed his father Ali Khamenei. Only AI-generated images and purported written messages have emerged, raising questions about who is actually running Iran.

item.title: 🛢️💰 Saudi Aramco Profit Jumps 25% as Pipeline Bypasses Hormuz Disruption
item.description: The world's largest oil company reported $32.5 billion in Q1 profit as it shifted exports to its East-West Pipeline running to the Red Sea. Brent crude stands at $103.91/barrel, below war highs but still elevated from pre-war $70 levels.

item.title: 🇮🇷 Jailed Iranian Nobel laureate Narges Mohammadi hospitalized after medical crisis
item.description: The Iranian activist and Nobel Peace Prize winner was transferred to a hospital after collapsing in prison over a week ago. Her family had been pleading for proper medical care.

item.title: 🇱🇻 Latvian defense minister resigns after drone incursions violate airspace
item.description: Defense Minister Andris Spruds resigned after drones violated Latvian airspace. Prime Minister Evika Silina requested his resignation, saying he had lost her trust and that of the public.

item.title: 🇵🇱 Poland finds military spy drone near Russian Kaliningrad border
item.description: Polish military police discovered a drone with Cyrillic markings near the border with Russia's Kaliningrad exclave. Preliminary findings suggest it was a reconnaissance drone with no combat capabilities.

item.title: 🇷🇺🇺🇸 Kremlin expects US envoys Witkoff and Kushner for peace talks quite soon
item.description: Russian officials said they expect US envoys Steve Witkoff and Jared Kushner to visit Moscow soon. Peace talks remain stalled as the Kremlin insists Ukraine must withdraw from Donbas first.

item.title: 🇹🇷 Turkey announces propellant breakthrough for Yıldırımhan long-range ballistic missile
item.description: The Turkish government has achieved advances in hypersonic technologies and liquid-propellant propulsion systems for its Yıldırımhan missile program after nearly a decade of development.

item.title: 🇭🇺 Hungary's New PM Apologizes to Those Wronged Under Orbán
item.description: Hungary's newly elected prime minister delivered an apology to citizens harmed during Viktor Orbán's 14-year rule, marking a dramatic shift in the country's political direction after the opposition's victory.

item.title: 🇸🇾⚖️ Cousin of Syria's Assad Faces Trial on Murder and Torture Charges
item.description: Atef Najib, cousin of former President Bashar al-Assad, faces at least 10 charges including murder, torture, and responsibility for massacres in a landmark trial marking one of the first prosecutions of the fallen regime's inner circle.

item.title: 🇺🇸🇨🇳🔭 US-China Telescope Rivalry Reaches South American Skies
item.description: The United States has pressed Argentina and Chile to review two Chinese telescope projects in the Andean deserts, raising concerns about security implications and potential setbacks for astronomical research.

item.title: 🇮🇳 Narendra Modi's BJP Wins West Bengal Election for First Time
item.description: India's ruling BJP has won the West Bengal state election for the first time, marking a significant political shift as Modi consolidates power in a state long held by opposition parties.

item.title: 🇭🇰🇬🇧 Hong Kong Dissident Nathan Law Says China Spies in UK Not Surprising
item.description: Hong Kong pro-democracy activist Nathan Law commented on Chinese intelligence operations in Britain, stating that the revelations of spy activity targeting dissidents abroad were expected given Beijing's pursuit of critics worldwide.

item.title: 🇮🇷📈 Food Inflation Devastates Iranian Households Amid War
item.description: Iranians face skyrocketing food prices as inflation soars and the currency plunges under a US-enforced naval blockade, with basic staples becoming increasingly unaffordable for ordinary citizens.

item.title: 🇺🇸🇲🇦 Remains of Missing US Soldier Recovered Off Morocco Coast
item.description: The remains of 1st Lt Kendrick Lamont Key Jr, who went missing during military exercises involving US, NATO allies and African nations, have been recovered off the coast of Morocco.

item.title: 🇩🇪🇺🇸 Germany moves to purchase US Tomahawk missiles for defense boost
item.description: Berlin is reviving efforts to acquire American Tomahawk cruise missiles as part of a broader European push to strengthen defense capabilities amid growing security concerns on the continent.

item.title: 🇫🇷🌍 Macron leads diplomatic push in East Africa to redefine French influence
item.description: The French president is touring Kenya and East African nations for an Africa summit, seeking to reshape France's role on the continent amid changing geopolitical dynamics and competition from other powers.

item.title: 🇱🇧 Hezbollah strikes Iron Dome battery with FPV drone on northern border
item.description: Hezbollah released footage of a First-Person View drone striking an Iron Dome battery, highlighting the IDF struggle against FPV drones that use cables and manual operation to evade jamming technologies.

item.title: 🇰🇷 South Korea identifies aircraft that struck cargo ship in Hormuz
item.description: Seoul says two unidentified aircraft struck the HMM Namu cargo ship in the Strait of Hormuz, causing fire and smoke. Iran denied responsibility for the attack.

item.title: 🇺🇦 Ukraine ramps up ground robot production to evacuate civilians and soldiers
item.description: Ukraine contracted 25,000 ground robots in first half of 2026, more than double last year. UGVs are now dual-use: hauling ammo, evacuating wounded, and rescuing civilians from contested zones like Lyman.
